message:
fix(sync): propagate auto_rebuilt flag and short-circuit redundant --rebuild/--force after open-time recovery

The open-storage path can rebuild the SQLite db from JSONL in three
recovery cases (db file missing, recoverable post-open anomaly,
open failure with successful fall-through to JSONL recovery). When
that happens, the db is already a clean import of the JSONL — there
is nothing left for `br sync --rebuild` or `br sync --force` to do.
Until now the user-invoked rebuild ran anyway, exercising
`reset_data_tables` + bulk INSERT against a connection that had
just done the same import, which sometimes tripped fsqlite's
stale-pager bug:
    "OpenRead failed: could not open storage cursor on root page N"

src/config/mod.rs::open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery
  Change the return type from `Result<SqliteStorage>` to
  `Result<(SqliteStorage, bool)>`. The bool is `true` for each of
  the three recovery paths (db-missing, anomaly-triggered rebuild,
  open-failure fallback) and `false` for the happy path. Each
  callsite is updated to wrap its `Ok(storage)` in
  `Ok((storage, true|false))` accordingly. The bool then flows
  through `OpenStorageResult` (already widened in a previous pass
  to surface this kind of metadata).

src/cli/commands/sync.rs::execute
  Destructure `auto_rebuilt` from the OpenStorageResult and pass
  it into `execute_import`. The field is also added to the
  `info!("Import configuration resolved")` log so the rebuild
  decision shows up in tracing.

src/cli/commands/sync.rs::execute_import
  New short-circuit at the top of the function: when
  `auto_rebuilt && (args.rebuild || args.force)`, skip the redundant
  rebuild path entirely. Build a synthetic `ImportResultOutput`
  with `created = count_issues_in_jsonl(jsonl_path)` and emit the
  same human/JSON output the real import path would have produced
  (rich, plain, and JSON paths all covered, plus the
  `suppress_human_sync_output` gate). The `info!` line records
  the short-circuit ("Skipping redundant --rebuild/--force import:
  database was just rebuilt from JSONL during open"). Returns
  `Ok(())` immediately — the cleanup that follows in the normal
  path is unnecessary because the import really did happen, just
  during open.

Side note: `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]` is added to the
function attribute list because adding `auto_rebuilt` brings the
arg count back over clippy's threshold; the previous allow tagged
only `clippy::too_many_lines`.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust
passes. Untracked `fix_test_locks.sh` is intentionally not staged.
